concerns.text: Task T028 states 'load acoustic baseline data', which silently relaxes FR-005 and Plan Summary item 6. The spec explicitly requires the acoustic baseline to be 'constructed by re-running the original EVA-Bench pipeline on the same dataset with acoustic perturbations'. Loading pre-existing data violates the Data Hygiene principle (Const-III) and the Single Source of Truth (Const-IV) by potentially bypassing the required re-execution step.

Task T015 limits the batch processing loop to '1000ms delay' to verify tractability. This is a semantic weakening of FR-001 and Const-VI, which mandate the system MUST implement injection across the full range of '200ms to 2000ms in 200ms increments'. A task scoped only to a single point (1000ms) does not satisfy the requirement to implement the full sweep capability.

Task T023 handles the fallback to linear regression but omits the specific acceptance criterion from SC-002: 'explicitly report... with a confidence interval'. The task description only mentions reporting 'No distinct threshold detected', dropping the mandatory statistical reporting requirement for the negative case.

Task T032 requires generating a conclusion based on 'ANOVA results', but FR-008 and SC-003 specifically mandate a 'statistical comparison (interaction test or t-test)' to validate distinctness. Relying solely on general ANOVA results without the specific interaction/t-test step is a relaxation of the validation requirement.
